revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
[[en:Chichibu Shrine]][[fr:Chichibu-jinja]][[ja:秩父神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Chichibu Shrine
秩父神社 | image = File:Chichibu-Shrine.JPG | caption = The ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' (prayer hall) | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.9975|N|139.08416667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|qid=Q812767|1=Shinto}} | type = | deity ={{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}; {{ill|Omoikane|qid=Q586088|1=Omoikane}}; {{ill|Q106611352|qid=Q106611352|1=Chichibuhiko no mikoto}}; {{ill|Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu|qid=Q700317|1=Yasuhito, Prince Chichibu}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 1-3 Banba-machi, Chichibu, Saitama 368-0041 | website = {{URL|http://www.chichibu-jinja.or.jp/|qq=}} | architecture_style ={{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|qid=Q3155268|1=Ishi-no-ma-zukuri}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Chichibu|qid=Q647079|1=Chichibu, Saitama}} }} The {{Nihongo|'''Chichibu Shrine'''|秩父神社|Chichibu-jinja|qq=}} is a Japanese {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} at {{ill|Chichibu, Saitama|en|Chichibu, Saitama|qid=Q647079|lt=Chichibu|1=Chichibu, Saitama}} in {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}.Kotodamaya.com, [http://www.kotodamaya.com/chichibu-jinja/ "Chichibu Jinja"]; retrieved 2013-1-26. ==History== According to text in the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' (''[[Kujiki]]''), Chichibuhiko-no-mikoto, the tenth-generation descendant of the [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Chichibu Province|en|Chichibu Province|qid=Q5096034|lt=Chichibu Province|1=Chichibu Province}}, established the shrine in the tenth year of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} to worship [[Omoikane|Yagokoro-omoikane-no-mikoto]]. The shrine contains * {{Nihongo|[[Omoikane|Yagokoroomoikane no mikoto]]|八意思兼命|||qq=}} * {{Nihongo|[[:en:Chichibuhiko no mikoto|Chichibuhiko no mikoto]]|知知夫彦命|||qq=}} * {{Nihongo|[[Ame-no-Minakanushi]]-no-kami|天之御中主神|||qq=}} * {{Nihongo|[[:en:Prince Chichibu|Prince Chichibu]]|秩父宮雍仁親王|Chichibu-no-Miya Yasuhito-shinnō||qq=}} During the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine merged with a neighboring temple, and was known as Myōken-gū until the separation of Shinto and Buddhism (''[[Shinbutsu bunri]]'') in the late-19th century.[http://www.chichibu-jinja.or.jp/saijin/index.htm 社殿 ご由緒 at Chichibu-jinja.or.jp]; retrieved 2013-1-26. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was one of 34 sacred sites of the old Chichibu ProvinceHarold Bolitho. (2003). [https://books.google.com/books?id=OksPvlsQOZEC&dq=chichibu+province&pg=PA40 "Tokugawa Japan's Tourist Revolution,"] ''Treasures of the Yenching: Seventy-fifth Anniversary of the Harvard-Yenching Library,'' p. 40. or [[Chichibu District]].[http://www.sainokuni-kanko.jp/eng/sightseeingspot/017.html "Chichibu Shrine" at Sainokuni-kanko.jp] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130919161424/http://www.sainokuni-kanko.jp/eng/sightseeingspot/017.html |date=2013-09-19 |qq=}}; retrieved 2013-1-26. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} it took the name Chichbu Shrine, with the characters 知知夫神社 appearing on the tablet of the ''[[torii]]''. In the system of Ranked Shinto shrines, Chichibu was listed among the 3rd class of nationally significant shrines or {{nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kokuhei-shōsha]]''|国幣小社|qq=}}.{{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1963). ''The vicissitudes of Shinto,'' p. 288. The shrine's grounds include a number of subsidiary shrines. A [[Tenmangū|Tenjin Shrine]], {{ill|Tōshō-gū|en|Tōshō-gū|qid=Q1190456|lt=Tōshō-gū|1=Tōshō-gū}}, and a [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Shrine]] are among them. ==Events== [[File:ChichibuFes1.jpg|thumb|Chichibu's annual night festival]] The shrine hosts a number of events. The rice-planting festival takes place annually on April 4. The mid-summer Kawase Festival is celebrated on July 19 and 20. The Banba-machi Suwa Shrine festival is not annual, but occasional. It resembles the {{ill|Onbashira|en|Onbashira|qid=Q3698846|lt=Onbashira|1=Onbashira}} festival of the [[Suwa-taisha|Suwa Taisha]]. Chichibu's annual night festival draws the biggest crowds. Celebrated in December, it spans the first six days of the month. The parading of floats is listed as part of the national cultural heritage.[http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp 国指定文化財　データベース:検索結果一覧] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120319233125/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp |date=2012-03-19 |qq=}} Retrieved August 23, 2008[http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080730003006/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp |date=2008-07-30 |qq=}}　データベース :主情報詳細 秩父祭屋台 Retrieved August 23, 2008 The national government has also recognized the shrine's sacred dance (''[[kagura]]'') as an important cultural asset.[http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp 国指定文化財　データベース:主情報詳細] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080730003006/http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp |date=2008-07-30 |qq=}} 秩父神社神楽 Retrieved on August 23, 2008 Similar honors have been awarded to its festival music, the floats, buildings and other possessions. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{Reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Chichibu-jinja|qq=}} *[http://www.chichibu-jinja.or.jp/ 秩父神社] (''Chichibu Jinja'') website] {{Shinto shrine|qq=}} <{{Myōken Faith|qq=}} {{Beppyo Shrines|qq=}} [[image:Chichibu-jinja Tenjin-chigi-sha.jpeg|thumb|The Tenjin-chigisha is located on the grounds of the Chichibu Shrine.]] [[image:Chichibu-jinja onbashira.jpeg|thumb|The ''onbashira'' is the focus of the Suwa Shrine festival.]] [[en:Chichibu Shrine]] {{Commons category|Chichibu-jinja}} [[fr:Chichibu-jinja]] [[ja:秩父神社]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Chichibu Shrine (Q2963366)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Chichibu District, Saitama|en|Chichibu District, Saitama|ja|秩父郡|ceb|Chichibu-gun|de|Chichibu-gun|fa|شهرستان چیچیبو، سایتاما|fr|District de Chichibu|id|Distrik Chichibu, Saitama|it|Distretto di Chichibu|javoyage|秩父郡|ko|지치부군|lt|Čičibu apskritis|pl|Powiat Chichibu|th|อำเภอชิจิบุ|uk|Повіт Тітібу|zh|秩父郡|lt=Chichibu district|qid=Q1145581|1=Chichibu District, Saitama}} || {{ill|Chichibu Shrine|lt=Chichibu Shrine|en|Chichibu Shrine|ja|秩父神社|commons|Category:Chichibu-jinja|fr|Chichibu-jinja|qid=Q2963366|1=Chichibu Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || {{ill|Shi-no-Miya|qid=Q134917307|lt=Shi-no-Miya|1=Shi-no-Miya}} || — || {{coord|35.997876|139.083999|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181726 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q2963366}} [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shi-no-Miya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Saitama Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:秩父市の神社]] [[Category:秩父神社]]
